data.description:

Marizyme ( (MRZM) ) has provided an update.

On April 9, 2026, Marizyme, Inc.’s board determined that transferring all or substantially all of the company’s assets through an assignment for the benefit of creditors was in its best interests and authorized execution of a general assignment agreement with assignee Peter Hurwitz. The company entered into this Assignment Agreement on April 14, 2026, and the assignee plans to commence the state court assignment proceeding in Palm Beach County, Florida, on or before April 17, 2026, marking a major shift in Marizyme’s operations and signaling severe financial distress with significant implications for creditors and other stakeholders.

More about Marizyme

Marizyme, Inc. is a healthcare company operating in the medical technology and life sciences sector. The firm focuses on developing and commercializing specialized therapeutic and diagnostic solutions, positioning itself within niche medical markets that rely on advanced clinical products and innovation-driven growth.

The latest announcement is out from Auna S.A. Class A ( (AUNA) ).

On April 17, 2026, Auna S.A. released preliminary key performance indicators for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, detailing volumes across its Mexican, Peruvian, and Colombian operations. The disclosure, which the company plans to repeat each quarter, signals a push toward greater transparency for investors by providing regular visibility into trends in emergency treatments, surgeries, hospital days, capacity utilization, oncology sessions, and plan memberships, offering a more granular view of operating momentum beyond headline financials.

The first-quarter data show steady or rising utilization in several key areas, including higher capacity use in Peru and Colombia and continued growth in Peru’s OncoSalud plan memberships and Colombia’s protected lives, underscoring the scale of Auna’s patient base. These metrics indicate that core service lines remain active and that its subscription-style health products are expanding, which may support perceptions of operational resilience and recurring revenue potential among shareholders and bondholders monitoring the company’s regional growth trajectory.

data.description:

Graphex Group ADR ( (GRFXY) ) just unveiled an update.

Graphex Group Limited has moved to voluntarily terminate its sponsored American Depositary Shares programme in the United States, where its ADSs currently trade on the OTC Expert Market under the symbol GRFXY. The decision follows years of declining U.S. listing status, including the May 2025 delisting of its ADSs from NYSE American and their migration to over-the-counter trading.

On April 10, 2026, the company instructed The Bank of New York Mellon to end the deposit agreement, and the depositary notified ADS holders on April 16, 2026 that the programme will terminate on July 14, 2026. Investors have until at least July 17, 2026 to surrender ADSs for the underlying Hong Kong–listed ordinary shares, after which the depositary may sell remaining shares and remit net proceeds, while Graphex will focus its listing and fundraising activities on its primary Hong Kong market.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GRFXY is a Neutral.

Graphex Group ADR’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its weak financial performance and poor valuation metrics. The technical analysis further indicates bearish momentum, with the stock trading below key moving averages and nearing oversold conditions. The lack of profitability and absence of a dividend yield make the stock less attractive to investors.

To see Spark’s full report on GRFXY stock, click here.

More about Graphex Group ADR

Graphex Group Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company (HKSE: 6128) whose ordinary shares trade on the Main Board of The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The company previously accessed U.S. capital markets through a sponsored American Depositary Share programme, with ADSs representing its Hong Kong–listed ordinary shares and having been traded on venues including NYSE American and, more recently, the OTC Expert Market.

GMEX ROBOTICS ( (GMEX) ) has issued an update.

GMEX Robotics Corporation filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on April 17, 2026, providing management’s discussion and analysis of financial condition and results of operations for the six-month periods ended December 31, 2025 and 2024. The filing also includes unaudited consolidated financial statements for those same periods, which are incorporated by reference into the company’s existing shelf registration statement on Form F-3, potentially streamlining future capital-raising activities and enhancing transparency for investors monitoring its performance and risk profile.

The disclosure reiterates a wide range of operational, market, regulatory, and governance risks tied to GMEX Robotics’ business, from macroeconomic dependence and supply chain exposure to data security, competition in the fitness equipment sector, and challenges of operating as an emerging growth, foreign private issuer. These risk factors frame how stakeholders should interpret the newly furnished financial information, underlining the uncertainties that could affect the company’s future results, trading liquidity, and the protections available to shareholders given its Cayman Islands incorporation and Australian operating base.

On April 13, 2026, Lyra Therapeutics, Inc. agreed with RVAC Medicines (US), Inc. to terminate its sublease for about 23,704 rentable square feet at 880 Winter Street in Waltham, Massachusetts, originally signed on December 21, 2023. data.title: Vaxart Secures Flexible $25 Million Equity Purchase Facility
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Vaxart ( (VXRT) ).

On April 16, 2026, Vaxart, Inc. entered into a purchase agreement and related registration rights agreement with Lincoln Park Capital Fund, under which Lincoln Park committed to buy up to $25 million of Vaxart common stock over a 24‑month period, subject to specified conditions and a 4.99% ownership cap. Vaxart issued 447,067 shares as commitment consideration, and any additional sales will rely on an effective SEC registration statement and will be priced at formula-based discounts to prevailing market prices, with no warrants, rights of first refusal or participation rights attached.

The structure gives Vaxart sole discretion over the timing and size of stock sales, including potential regular and accelerated purchases, while obligating Lincoln Park to buy shares and prohibiting it from short selling or hedging the stock. The company expects to use any proceeds for working capital, capital expenditures and general corporate purposes, framing the facility as a flexible way to strengthen its balance sheet and support the advancement of its oral vaccine programs without immediately diluting shareholders through a large, one‑time equity raise.

The most recent analyst rating on (VXRT) stock is a Buy with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Vaxart stock, see the VXRT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VXRT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VXRT is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by improved financial performance (TTM profitability and positive cash flow) and constructive earnings-call factors (partnership validation, strong 2025 revenue, and runway extension), tempered by concerns about durability given contract-driven revenue and historical volatility. Technical signals are mixed and do not currently indicate strong near-term momentum, while valuation looks supportive but depends on earnings sustainability.

To see Spark’s full report on VXRT stock, click here.

More about Vaxart

Vaxart, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing oral recombinant vaccines delivered in pill form using its proprietary platform. Its pipeline targets coronavirus, norovirus and influenza prevention, as well as a therapeutic human papillomavirus vaccine, with a focus on room‑temperature stable, needle‑free products supported by broad patent filings.

Vaxart’s pill-based vaccines are designed to simplify storage, shipping and administration versus traditional injectable vaccines. The company aims to create oral versions of existing vaccines and new candidates for emerging indications, positioning itself within the competitive global vaccines market.

The latest update is out from Tri Pointe ( (TPH) ).

Tri Pointe Homes, Inc. reported that the mandatory waiting period under the Hart-Scott-Rodino Antitrust Improvements Act for its proposed merger with Japan’s Sumitomo Forestry Co., Ltd. expired at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time on April 16, 2026. This expiration satisfies a key U.S. antitrust condition for the deal, in which a Sumitomo subsidiary will merge into Tri Pointe, leaving Tri Pointe as the surviving corporation and an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of the Japanese parent.

The merger remains subject to other closing conditions, including those outlined in the companies’ merger agreement, so completion is not yet assured. The company also highlighted a range of risks that could affect the transaction’s outcome, including potential delays, termination scenarios, litigation, supply chain pressures and broader macroeconomic and trade-related headwinds that could influence its operations and deal execution.

data.title: Virtus Investment Partners Updates Non-GAAP Reporting Framework
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Virtus Investment Partners ( (VRTS) ) has issued an announcement.

Virtus Investment Partners said it will revise certain non-GAAP financial measures beginning with its first-quarter 2026 results to include tax benefits realized from the amortization of goodwill and intangible assets, which had previously been excluded. The change will lower the reported non-GAAP effective tax rate and increase adjusted diluted earnings per share and adjusted net income for past and future periods, with management arguing the new definitions better capture the economic impact of acquisition-driven tax assets and provide investors with a clearer view of underlying performance without altering previously filed GAAP results.

To support comparability, the firm is publishing supplemental reconciliations for the first quarter of 2024 through the fourth quarter of 2025, plus full-year 2024 and 2025, showing how the revised metrics differ from the historical non-GAAP calculations. The updated framework continues to strip out items such as non-cash amortization, restructuring costs, acquisition and integration expenses, deferred compensation volatility and seed-capital investment gains or losses, while emphasizing that these adjusted measures have limitations and should be considered alongside audited GAAP figures.

The most recent analyst rating on (VRTS) stock is a Sell with a $128.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Virtus Investment Partners stock, see the VRTS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VRTS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VRTS is a Neutral.

The score is held back mainly by volatile/negative recent cash flow and weak technical trend signals (price below major moving averages, negative MACD). These are partially offset by attractive valuation (low P/E and high dividend yield) and a mixed-but-stabilizing earnings narrative featuring ETF momentum, cost discipline, and solid liquidity despite continued outflow pressure.

To see Spark’s full report on VRTS stock, click here.

More about Virtus Investment Partners

Virtus Investment Partners, Inc. is a U.S.-based asset management firm that provides investment management and related services. The company has expanded through numerous acquisitions over several years, creating a sizable intangible tax asset that generates meaningful tax benefits tied to goodwill and intangible amortization, which has become increasingly important to its financial profile.

An update from Getty Images Holdings ( (GETY) ) is now available.

On October 27, 2023, a U.S. District Court in New York ruled in favor of Alta Partners and CRCM Institutional Master Fund on certain breach of contract claims related to Getty Images Holdings’ public warrants, awarding approximately $36.9 million to Alta and $51.0 million to CRCM plus pre-judgment interest, while ruling in favor of the company on other significant claims, including Alta’s claims on later-acquired warrants. The Second Circuit Court of Appeals affirmed this judgment in all respects on January 15, 2026, and after the appeals court denied Getty Images’ rehearing request on April 16, 2026, the company confirmed it had already recorded a litigation loss and maintained a reserve for the full amount and will partially draw on its existing revolving credit facility, via subsidiary Getty Images, Inc., to help fund payment of the judgment and interest.

An announcement from Orasure Technologies ( (OSUR) ) is now available.

On April 16, 2026, OraSure Technologies announced a cooperation agreement with activist investor Altai Capital, under which Altai withdrew prior director nominations and stockholder proposals and agreed to customary standstill and voting provisions through March 31, 2027. As part of the deal, OraSure appointed healthcare technology executive John D. Bertrand as an independent Class II director, added him to its Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee and committed to include him on its 2026 director slate, while capping board size at eight members before the 2027 annual meeting.

The board also resolved on April 16, 2026 to seek shareholder approval at the 2026 annual meeting to declassify its board structure and phase in annual director elections, a move positioned as strengthening governance, oversight and shareholder engagement. The agreement formalizes a more collaborative relationship with Altai, including regular meetings on financial and strategic matters, signaling a negotiated end to a potential proxy contest and reinforcing OraSure’s broader multi-year transformation and value-creation strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (OSUR) stock is a Hold with a $3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Orasure Technologies stock, see the OSUR Stock Forecast page.

More about Orasure Technologies

OraSure Technologies, Inc., listed on Nasdaq as OSUR, is a healthcare diagnostics company specializing in point-of-need and home diagnostic tests and sample management solutions. Through subsidiaries including DNA Genotek, Sherlock Biosciences and BioMedomics, it develops, manufactures and distributes rapid diagnostic tests and sample collection and stabilization devices sold globally to clinical, public health, research, government, pharmaceutical and direct-to-consumer markets.

The company focuses on decentralizing diagnostics to connect people to healthcare wherever they are, aiming to improve access, quality and value of care through effortless testing and sample management innovations. Its products target the discovery and detection of critical medical conditions and are used by clinical laboratories, hospitals, physicians’ offices and community organizations worldwide.

data.title: PMGC Holdings Secures $40 Million Equity Purchase Facility
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

PMGC Holdings ( (ELAB) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 16, 2026, PMGC Holdings Inc. entered into a securities purchase agreement with an institutional investor establishing a $40 million equity purchase facility structured as pre-paid share purchases. An initial tranche of about $10 million, carrying an original issue discount and associated costs, was scheduled to close around April 17, 2026, providing net proceeds of roughly $9.7 million and giving the investor certain participation rights in future financings and a secured, first-priority claim on subsidiary equity.

The 24‑month facility, which runs through April 16, 2028, allows PMGC to draw additional capital at its discretion, subject to caps, pricing floors tied to Nasdaq rules, and weekly sale limitations on the investor’s share disposals. Backed by guarantees from PMGC’s operating subsidiaries and a pledge of their equity, the arrangement gives the company flexible, committed funding to accelerate its roll-up of U.S. precision manufacturing assets in aerospace, defense, and industrial markets, supporting further acquisitions and operational investments while diluting existing shareholders through new stock issuance.

The company also committed to reserve additional shares, secure stockholder approval for issuances above Nasdaq’s 19.99% cap, and maintain covenants that favor the investor in future variable-rate transactions. For stakeholders, the facility strengthens PMGC’s acquisition firepower and balance-sheet flexibility but concentrates security interests over key assets with the investor and embeds participation and consent rights that could influence the structure and terms of future financings.

Spark’s Take on ELAB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ELAB is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (volatile revenue, large operating losses, and sustained cash burn). Technicals also remain bearish (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Corporate events provide some offset via strategic expansion and contracts/compliance wins, but financing-related risk and the lack of profitability keep the overall score low.

To see Spark’s full report on ELAB stock, click here.

More about PMGC Holdings

PMGC Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: ELAB) is a diversified holding company executing a targeted roll-up strategy in U.S.-based precision manufacturing, with a current focus on acquiring aerospace, defense, and industrial manufacturing businesses. Its portfolio includes ITAR-registered, AS9100D-certified precision CNC machining operations and a specialty IT hardware packaging company serving data center and AI infrastructure markets.

The company aims to build a scaled, vertically integrated precision manufacturing platform serving mission-critical supply chains across defense, aerospace, and next-generation infrastructure. PMGC seeks growth through acquisitions, operational integration, and investments in automation, workforce development, and advanced manufacturing capabilities, while also opportunistically buying cash-generative businesses outside its core aerospace and defense focus when returns are attractive.

Average Trading Volume: 7,297,876

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Rockwell Automation ( (ROK) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 15, 2026, Rockwell Automation’s board approved an expansion of its board size from ten to eleven directors, effective April 16, 2026, as part of its governance framework under the company’s by-laws. The move reflects an effort to broaden board oversight at a time when industrial automation continues to play a central role in global manufacturing and digital transformation.

Also on April 15, 2026, the board elected David A. Zapico as a director effective April 16, 2026, assigning him to a board class with a term expiring at the 2027 annual meeting of shareowners. Zapico was appointed to the Compensation and Talent Management Committee and the Board Composition and Corporate Governance Committee, and will receive standard non-employee director equity and cash compensation, underscoring Rockwell’s adherence to established, arms-length director compensation and selection practices.

Fifth Third Bancorp ( (FITB) ) has shared an announcement.

Fifth Third Bancorp reported first-quarter 2026 results showing strong underlying business momentum, with net interest income rising to $1.94 billion and noninterest income to $895 million, although certain items reduced reported earnings per share to $0.15 diluted. Core metrics improved, including a 17 basis point sequential expansion in net interest margin to 3.30%, 15% year-over-year growth in tangible book value per share and better credit quality, as net charge-offs fell to 0.37% and the nonperforming asset ratio declined to 0.57%.

The company closed its acquisition of Comerica on February 1, 2026, adding $86 billion in assets, $51 billion in loans and $65 billion in deposits, which significantly boosted average loans and deposits in the quarter. Management highlighted stability in funding, with demand deposits rising to 28% of total deposits, improved profitability ratios and strong deposit and fee revenue growth, while integration of Comerica’s operations and teams is progressing as planned, supporting Fifth Third’s expanded scale and market presence, including Texas through letters of intent for 81 branch locations.

The most recent analyst rating on (FITB) stock is a Buy with a $55.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fifth Third Bancorp stock, see the FITB Stock Forecast page.
